title: who is actually the second angel
display_title: who is actually the second angel
wikitext:
[]: who is actually the second angel.             Quote Post by myheadexplode » Thu Dec 15, 2005 6:57 pm im really confused about this matter. It have heard that Lilith is the source of life of humanity and all of the angels. Adam is also stated to be the first angel. How can he be the first angel if Lilith is the source of all the angels. I can only assume that Adam and Lilith were created around the same time or something like that. Does anyone have any answers or ideas

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Thu Dec 15, 2005 7:00 pm Adam and Lilith were the first two "Angels". Adam was the source of the rest of the Angels, except humanity, who came from Lilith. The Eva game for the PS2 revealed that Lilith and Adam were both created by an alien civilisation, IIRC, and seeded on earth. Go figure.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Dec 15, 2005 7:42 pm This is confusion derived from poor translation on the part of Manga Entertainment. The line in the movie is: "Human beings were born from a being called Lilith, just like Adam." Although it should read something like this: "Human beings were born from a being like Adam called Lilith." Lilith and Adam are both sources of life. Lilith spawned Humanity. Adam spawned the Angels. They are seeds of an alien civilization, possibly even the same civilization. The order of the Angels has nothing to do with age. They are numbered in the order that they are identified. As the supposed "Tabris" indicates, even the number of Angels itself is sketchy, as Tabris is actually Adam, and should not be counted twice, as the first and seventeenth Angel.

[]:             Quote Post by Thome » Fri Dec 23, 2005 6:38 pm Technically when you look at the Book of Enoch, it states that Adam came first and Lilith after. So technically Lillith is the second, however it really doesn't matter in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Dec 23, 2005 7:58 pm Thome wrote: Technically when you look at the Book of Enoch...

[]:             Quote Post by Thome » Fri Dec 23, 2005 8:08 pm Oh, really? Is the book of Enoch that irrellivant? I've seen more then enough evidence to suggest that Eva is closly based off of The Book of Enoch, and most of it's sister tomes(or whatever you want to call them :P) However as I said, the order of which Adam and Lilith appeared doesn't really matter to the series. And Adam doesn't even matter at all in the series. After adam's genetic mattierial was used in the creation of the Eva series he(it) was reduced to nothing but an embryo-like state. He had next to no power at all. I'm still wondering why Gendo felt it nessesary to inject Adam into his hand.....

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Dec 23, 2005 9:03 pm Closely based on Enoch, closely based on the Bible, closely based on Freud, closely based on scientology... Heard many of them 'based on'. Yet, Eva's universe is not based specifically on any of these. And thus the external references can't provide any certainty (sp?) when taken on that universe. Reality != Eva; Myths/religions != Eva; Science != Eva. The only certain answers come from Eva itself, not from externalities. That's the first step, I believe, in understanding Eva: leaving the external references aside. Besides, "based" != "is".

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Dec 24, 2005 2:13 am AchtungAffen wrote: The only certain answers come from Eva itself, not from externalities. That's the first step, I believe, in understanding Eva: leaving the external references aside.

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Tue Dec 27, 2005 12:37 am Space Penis wrote: Adam and Lilith were the first two "Angels". Adam was the source of the rest of the Angels, except humanity, who came from Lilith. The Eva game for the PS2 revealed that Lilith and Adam were both created by an alien civilisation, IIRC, and seeded on earth. Go figure.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Dec 27, 2005 3:55 am TheBlueTree wrote: but that whole alien thingy was total bs right?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Tue Dec 27, 2005 9:42 am TheBlueTree wrote: but that whole alien thingy was total bs right?

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Tue Dec 27, 2005 5:33 pm Its doesnt mean aliens sent them, I think he ment by god/creator of universe thingy.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Dec 27, 2005 10:56 pm TheBlueTree wrote: Its doesnt mean aliens sent them, I think he ment by god/creator of universe thingy.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Wed Dec 28, 2005 7:34 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: TheBlueTree wrote: Its doesnt mean aliens sent them, I think he ment by god/creator of universe thingy.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Dec 28, 2005 3:36 pm Another possibility is that in the beginning there was only one, which divided and thus the 2 SoL were created. As you can see, there are many possibilities. I prefer the FAR one, but until I get to see that information confirmed, I won't put my hands in the fire for it.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Wed Dec 28, 2005 3:45 pm If you listen to the "commentary of evil" while watcing EoE they do say something about Adam and Lilith dividing like cells do, and having conjugational reproduction for the first time in history. But its just the commentary of evil with AWL so I wouldn't put to much faith in it.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Dec 28, 2005 7:27 pm I got that theory from somewhere else very far away from ADV releases. But, if they thought the same then it's probably wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Sat Dec 31, 2005 1:00 am Space Penis wrote: ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: TheBlueTree wrote: Its doesnt mean aliens sent them, I think he ment by god/creator of universe thingy.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sat Dec 31, 2005 1:02 pm Sounds better than the giant ameoba theory to me.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Jan 01, 2006 11:41 pm All true believers of course know that Third Impact was entirely at the behest of the Flying Spagehetti Monster. Complementation was mearly the mass carasses of his noodly appendage!
